title: Identify parallel and perpendicular lines
description: Recognize parallel and perpendicular lines in geometric figures and pictures.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these figures shows parallel lines?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Parallel lines}}$ are always the same distance apart from each other — no matter how far we extend them, they will never meet.

[[☃ image 1]]

Which figure shows *parallel lines*?
This figure shows *parallel* lines:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![Two lines that meet at right angles.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/86975c7968fc3e94825df93eb44b1b0d15a8f14d)
![Two lines that are the same distance apart from each other and will never intersect.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a8adf8dbb35ca7b96223a7e4a8a6e867f692cfe)
![Two lines that intersect each other.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4578b30eaf94525a86de3a95953e08df5dda586d)

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the map below, then answer the question based on the appearance of the lines on the map. 

**Which statement about Main Street and Pine Street is correct? **

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Parallel lines}}$ are always the same distance apart from each other — no matter how far we extend them, they will never meet.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenE{\text{Perpendicular lines}}$ are lines that meet at right angles.  



[[☃ image 2]]

We can compare these to the lines formed by Main Street and Pine Street.

Looking at the map, we can see that Main Street and Pine Street intersect, or meet at an angle. 

Parallel lines never meet, no matter how far we extend them, so the two streets are **not** parallel. 



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also see that Main Street and Pine Street do **not** meet at a right angle, so the two streets are not perpendicular.
Main Street is neither parallel nor perpendicular to Pine Street.

options.alt: A street map. Holly Street and Pine Street are the same distance apart from each other and will never meet. Main Street intersects both Holly Street and Pine Street at an angle to both. Alta Street intersects Holly Street and Pine Street at right angles to both.

choices.content: Main Street is parallel to Pine Street.
Main Street is perpendicular to Pine Street.
Main Street is neither parallel nor perpendicular to Pine Street.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the map below, then answer the question based on the appearance of the lines on the map. 

**Which statement about $2\text{nd}$ Street and Market Street is correct? **

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Parallel lines}}$ are always the same distance apart from each other — no matter how far we extend them, they will never meet.

[[☃ image 1]]


$\greenE{\text{Perpendicular lines}}$ are lines that meet at right angles.  

[[☃ image 2]]

We can compare these to the lines formed by $2\text{nd}$ Street and Market Street.

Looking at the map, we can see that $2\text{nd}$ Street and Market Street intersect, or meet at a right angle. 

[[☃ image 1]]
$2\text{nd}$ Street is perpendicular to Market Street.

options.alt: A street map. 1st Street and 2nd Street are the same distance apart from each other and will never meet. King Street intersects both 1st Street and 2nd Street at an angle to both. Market Street intersects 1st Street and 2nd Street at right angles to both.

choices.content: $2\text{nd}$ Street is parallel to Market Street.
$2\text{nd}$ Street is perpendicular to Market Street.
$2\text{nd}$ Street is neither parallel nor perpendicular to Market Street.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the map below, then answer the question based on the appearance of the lines on the map. 

**Which statement about Robin Street and Owl Street is correct? **



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\purpleD{\text{Parallel lines}}$ are always the same distance apart from each other — no matter how far we extend them, they will never meet.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenE{\text{Perpendicular lines}}$ are lines that meet at right angles.  


[[☃ image 2]]

We can compare these to the lines formed by Robin Street and Owl Street.

Looking at the map, we can see that Robin Street and Owl Street are always the same distance apart from each other — no matter how far we extend them, they will never meet.


[[☃ image 1]]
Robin Street is parallel to Owl Street.

options.alt: A street map. Robin Street and Owl Street are the same distance apart from each other and will never meet. Dove Street intersects both Robin Street and Owl Street at an angle to both. Red Street intersects Robin Street and Owl Street at an angle to both.

choices.content: Robin Street is parallel to Owl Street.
Robin Street is perpendicular to Owl Street.
Robin Street is neither parallel nor perpendicular to Owl Street.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the flag of Trinidad and Tobago below, then answer the question based on the appearance of the lines on the flag. 

**Which statement about the two white stripes in the flag of Trinidad and Tobago is correct? **




[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Below are examples of $\greenC{\text{a pair of perpendicular lines}}$ and $\purpleC{\text{a pair of parallel lines}}$.  


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

We can compare these to the white stripes in the flag of Trinidad and Tobago.

Looking at the flag, we can see that the white stripes do not meet to form angles. 

Perpendicular lines meet to form $90^\circ$ angles, so the white stripes are **not** perpendicular. 




[[☃ image 1]]

We can also see that the white stripes are always the same distance apart and do not meet.
The white stripes in the flag of Trinidad and Tobago are parallel to each other.

options.alt: The Trinidad and Tobago flag is red with a thick diagonal black stripe between two thin diagonal white stripes. Two white stripes are the same distance apart from each other and will never meet.

choices.content: The white stripes are parallel to each other.
The white stripes are perpendicular to each other.
The white stripes are neither parallel nor perpendicular to each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the flag of Aruba below, then answer the question based on the appearance of the lines on the flag. 

**Which statement about the two yellow stripes in the flag of Aruba is correct? **


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Below are examples of $\greenC{\text{a pair of perpendicular lines}}$ and $\purpleC{\text{a pair of parallel lines}}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


We can compare these to the yellow stripes in the flag of Aruba.

Looking at the flag, we can see that the yellow stripes do not meet to form angles. 

Perpendicular lines meet to form $90^\circ$ angles, so the yellow stripes are **not** perpendicular. 

[[☃ image 1]]



We can also see that the yellow stripes are always the same distance apart and do not meet.
The yellow stripes in the flag of Aruba are parallel to each other.

options.alt: The Aruba flag is blue with a red 4-point star outlined in white in the top left corner. Two yellow lines near the bottom are the same distance apart from each other and will never meet.

choices.content: The yellow stripes are parallel to each other.
The yellow stripes are perpendicular to each other.
The yellow stripes are neither parallel nor perpendicular to each other.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the flag of Sweden below, then answer the question based on the appearance of the lines on the flag. 

**Which statement about the two yellow stripes in the Swedish flag is correct? **


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Below are examples of $\greenC{\text{a pair of perpendicular lines}}$ and $\purpleC{\text{a pair of parallel lines}}$.  


[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

We can compare these to the yellow stripes in the flag of Sweden.

Looking at the flag, we can see that the yellow stripes intersect. 

Parallel lines never meet, no matter how far they are extended, so the two yellow stripes are **not** parallel. 



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also see that the yellow stripes meet at $90^\circ$ angles.
The two yellow stripes in the Swedish flag are perpendicular to each other.

options.alt: The flag of Sweden is blue with a yellow cross formed by two stripes. A vertical yellow stripe intersects with a horizontal yellow stripe at a right angle.

choices.content: The two yellow stripes in the Swedish flag are parallel to each other.
The two yellow stripes in the Swedish flag are perpendicular to each other.
The two yellow stripes in the Swedish flag are neither parallel nor perpendicular to each other.
